mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 1-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 250.5 indicates rising inflation, prompting the Fed to raise rates, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal a monetary tightening cycle amid inflation, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS signals higher discount rates, which reduce equity valuations and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of tightening periods despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger supply-side activity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tight labor markets are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts (HOUST) reflect stronger demand for residential construction, which stimulates economic activity and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods with rising HOUST despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month CPIAUCSL reading above 250.5 indicates persistent inflation, which may lead to tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.
